sections.instruction: Complete the sentences below for questions 11-15. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND OR A NUMBER for each answer. Choose the correct letter A, B or C for questions 16-18. Match the locations for questions 19-20.

questions.content: The Read for All charity gets most of its operating funds from
Jake suggests radio listeners can make a donation to the Read for All charity
options: B. by dropping into one of the charity’s centres

questions.content: The Read for All charity can sometimes pay
options: A. a modest hourly rate for people who work for them
B. for some overnight accommodation if volunteers live far from a centre
C. for workers’ expenses if they provide a receipt

sections.instruction: Complete the table below for questions 21-25. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer. Choose the correct letter A, B or C for questions 26-30.

questions.content: Why was Anna delayed starting her essay this month?
options: B. she had other work to prepare

questions.content: Where does Mr. Stevenson recommend that Anna study?
options: B. Mr. Stevenson’s dedicated study periods

questions.content: What does Mr. Stevenson ask Anna to provide him with for an extension?
options: A. a copy of the department rules
B. a paper from her doctor
C. an application form for the extension

questions.content: What was Anna’s first possible topic to write about?
options: A. foreign investment helped by reduces tax deals offered by the Australian government
B. Australian taxes invested in overseas investment
C. how foreign governments’ tax deals help Australian business investment overseas

questions.content: What was Anna’s second possible topic to write about?
options: A. the travel business in Australia and New Zealand
B. taxation issues between Australia and New Zealand
C. a history of trade between Australia and New Zealand

sections.instruction: Match the person with their actions (A-G) for questions 31-35. Complete the summary below for questions 36-40. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

options: A. stressed the importance of the link between handwriting and brain functions
B. was the first paid for his autograph
C. wrote the first signature in handwriting that we still have
D. used seals to identify ownership
F. first wrote about the science that was to be called graphology

questions.content:
The development of e-signature
E-signatures have made the hand-writing signature (36) {{BLANK}} Technologies such as fax and internet created a need for contracts and agreements to be signed without the presence of the signer. E-signatures did not satisfy previous basic law requirements for handwritten signatures as they did not create a (37) {{BLANK}} The UN created a (38) {{BLANK}} in 1996 that set out rules that allowed electronic business to go ahead as e-contracts and paper contracts would meet with (39) {{BLANK}} in law. Although online businesses can use click-to-agree there is still demand for an electronic hand written signature as it can help (40) {{BLANK}} in law and create human touch. This kind of hand written signature is available on software used to create electronic signatures.
